questions.q: What is a Bill of Exchange?
questions.answer: A Bill of Exchange is a written order by a drawer (maker) directing a drawee (payee) to pay a certain sum of money to a drawee or to the order of the drawer. It is a mode of payment used for making a payment of a sum of money at a future date.

questions.q: What is the difference between a Bill of Exchange and a Promissory Note?
questions.topic: Bills of Exchange vs Promissory Notes
questions.answer: A Bill of Exchange is a formal order to pay a sum of money while a Promissory Note is an unconditional promise in writing to pay a sum of money to a person. A Bill of Exchange is used for making payments while a Promissory Note is used for obtaining loan.

questions.q: What are the three essential parts of a Bill of Exchange?
questions.topic: Parts of a Bill of Exchange
questions.answer: The three essential parts of a Bill of Exchange are: Drawer, Drawee and Drawee's promise to pay. The drawer is the person who draws the bill, the drawee is the person who is required to pay the amount and the drawer's promise to pay is the unconditional promise to pay the amount.

questions.q: What is the difference between a Bill of Exchange and a Cheque?
questions.answer: A Bill of Exchange is a formal order to pay a sum of money while a Cheque is a bill of exchange drawn on a banker. A Bill of Exchange is used for making payments to non-bankers while a Cheque is used for making payments to bank accounts.
key_points: Payment to non-bankers vs payment to bank account

questions.q: What are the different types of Bills of Exchange?
questions.answer: The different types of Bills of Exchange are: Open Bill of Exchange, Usance Bill of Exchange, Sight Bill of Exchange and Clean Bill of Exchange. Each type has its own characteristics and uses.

questions.q: What is the difference between an Open Bill of Exchange and a Usance Bill of Exchange?
questions.topic: Open Bill of Exchange vs Usance Bill of Exchange
questions.answer: An Open Bill of Exchange is a bill that is payable on demand while a Usance Bill of Exchange is a bill that is payable at a future date. An Open Bill of Exchange has no credit period while a Usance Bill of Exchange has a credit period.
key_points: Payment on demand vs payment at future date

questions.q: What is the difference between a Sight Bill of Exchange and a Clean Bill of Exchange?
questions.topic: Sight Bill of Exchange vs Clean Bill of Exchange
questions.answer: A Sight Bill of Exchange is a bill that is payable on presentation while a Clean Bill of Exchange is a bill that is payable at a future date without any conditions. A Sight Bill of Exchange is used for immediate payment while a Clean Bill of Exchange is used for future payment.

questions.q: What is the process of accepting a Bill of Exchange?
questions.answer: The process of accepting a Bill of Exchange involves the drawee acknowledging the bill and promising to pay the amount to the holder. Acceptance is usually made by signing the bill in the presence of the drawer.

questions.q: What is the effect of a Bill of Exchange being dishonoured?
questions.answer: If a Bill of Exchange is dishonoured, the drawer can claim damages from the drawee. The holder of the bill can also claim damages from the drawer if he has accepted the bill. Dishonour of a bill is a breach of the drawee's promise to pay.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a Bill of Exchange in trade and commerce?
questions.topic: Role of Bill of Exchange in Trade and Commerce
questions.answer: The purpose of a Bill of Exchange in trade and commerce is to make payments at a future date. It is used to facilitate payment for goods and services sold on credit.
